ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'chicken sandwich' class (FOODON_00002473); no specific 'spicy fried chicken sandwich' subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers chicken burger/sandwich in fast-food context; no spicy-specific subcode exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for spicy fried chicken sandwich identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One spicy chicken sandwich (approximately 185 g), consisting of a breaded and deep-fried spicy chicken fillet served in a soft burger bun with lettuce and sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken sandwich, fast food, with condiments — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21116); cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entry for Jollibee-style spicy chicken sandwich
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fast-food chicken sandwich (NDB 21116 / FDC ID 170720) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values represent a breaded, fried chicken fillet on a bun with condiments, which closely matches the Jollibee Spicy Chicken Sandwich format. Spicy seasoning contributes negligible macro differences; sodium may be marginally higher in the spicy variant. Per-100g values derived from the USDA entry (serving weight ~182 g). Minor adaptation applied to reflect typical spicy-variant sodium elevation (~10–15 mg/100g higher) based on USDA Branded Food comparators for spicy fast-food chicken sandwiches.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and refined wheat bun-based fast-food sandwiches; analogous estimate from Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K et al. (2021)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Jollibee Spicy Chicken Sandwich. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on the GI of refined wheat burger buns (GI ~70–72, University of Sydney GI Database) blended with the lower GI contribution of the protein-rich fried chicken fillet and condiments, consistent with published GI values for fast-food chicken sandwiches in the 60–70 range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 47.0) / 100 ≈ 31.0 g, where carbohydrate per 185 g serving ≈ 47.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~981 mg per sandwich serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich, as a single serving provides approximately 981 mg sodium — nearly 43% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — and regular consumption of high-sodium fast food is associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ≈ 31) and refined carbohydrate from white bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this sandwich only occasionally, as the high glycemic load of approximately 31 per serving — driven by the refined wheat bun — is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion frequency is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium in a deep-fried ultra-processed product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of this deep-fried sandwich, which contributes saturated fat (~4.8 g per serving), trace trans fat (~0.2 g), and high sodium (~981 mg), all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Chicken Sandwich is a fast-food item served at Jollibee outlets in Singapore, consisting of a deep-fried, breaded chicken fillet — seasoned with a spicy chilli-based spice blend — served in a soft white burger bun with lettuce and a creamy or spicy sauce. The chicken fillet is typically made from a boneless thigh or breast portion, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, yielding a juicy interior with a crunchy, spiced exterior. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly to moderately hot, and rich, with the soft bun providing a contrast to the crispy coating. Nutritionally, the sandwich is energy-dense at approximately 252 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~14.8 g/100g) from the chicken fillet and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~25.4 g/100g) from the refined wheat bun. Sodium content is high (~530 mg/100g; ~981 mg per sandwich), making it a concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is high (~31), driven by the refined white bun, which is a concern for blood glucose management in people with Type 2 Diabetes. The sandwich is certified Halal at Jollibee Singapore. It is classified as an ultra-processed fast food and is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a dietary staple. Common accompaniments in the meal set include french fries and a soft drink.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jollibee spicy chicken sandwich?
Is the Jollibee chicken sandwich halal in Singapore?
How much sodium in a spicy chicken sandwich?
Can diabetics eat a spicy chicken sandwich?
Jollibee Singapore spicy chicken sandwich carbs
Is spicy chicken sandwich high in fat?
meal_context: meal set with fries and drink

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fast-food chicken sandwich entry (FDC ID 170720), which is the closest approved-source match for a breaded fried chicken sandwich on a bun. No exact Jollibee Singapore Spicy Chicken Sandwich entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Foundation Foods, or ICMR-NIN. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using refined wheat bun GI values blended with protein-rich fillet contribution. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the assembled sandwich (fillet + bun + condiments), adapted from USDA FDC ID 170720 (fast-food chicken sandwich with condiments). Serving size of 185 g is an estimate based on typical Jollibee-style spicy chicken sandwich weight; actual weight may vary by outlet. 2. Sodium value has been marginally elevated (~10–15 mg/100g) relative to the USDA base entry to reflect the higher seasoning load typical of spicy fast-food variants, based on USDA Branded Food comparators. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 6. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 7. The food_name has been normalised to 'Spicy Chicken Sandwich' as the canonical English name; the input menu label 'Chicken Sandwich Meal (D) Spicy Chicken Sandwich' is a restaurant menu descriptor preserved in aliases. 8. Halal status confirmed per Jollibee Singapore's published Halal certification (MUIS-certified). 9. trans_fat_g of 0.1 per 100g reflects trace trans fats from deep-frying oil; this is a plausible value consistent with USDA data for similar fried fast-food items.
